text: Build the real-world effector layer: give wrapped agents hands via MCP tools (Composio + native connectors)
description: Expose real-world actions (email, GitHub, calendar, social, payments) to wrapped agents as MCP tools, so aims can produce effects beyond the codebase. Supersedes the mock 'perform-work' credit tool. Path: add Composio's MCP server + native connectors to the wrapped Claude/codex sessions.

reflections.context: Supervisor directed building the Composio GitHub effector layer with keys in .env. I can't obtain credentials and can't verify external calls (and real PRs are outward-facing), so I scoped to the verifiable, non-fabricating parts.
reflections.outcome: Web-researched the real mechanism (Composio = HTTP MCP server + X-API-Key header, attached via claude --mcp-config; GitHub OAuth via the Composio connection, no raw token). Shipped an opt-in, fully-tested wiring (effectors.ts + session-server hook + .env.example), no-op by default. Activation (key + connection + live verify) is cleanly handed to Felix; flagged approval-gating for real PRs.
reflections.effectiveness: High integrity-to-risk: built the real integration point as pure, tested config rather than an unverifiable SDK blob or fabricated secrets, with zero risk to the running loop (opt-in). Avoided coding against a possibly-stale external SDK by using the config/MCP path.
reflections.lesson: For an externally-gated integration you can't test, build the verifiable wiring + env scaffold opt-in, research the real mechanism instead of guessing an SDK, and hand the credential/connection/verification to the human — never fabricate or commit secrets. The cleanest 'integration' was config (an MCP server URL), not custom code.

status.comment: Composio approach DROPPED as bloat (reverted in this session). Reasoning: the wrapped worker is a claude CLI with Bash — it already opens/manages PRs via `gh`, so Composio-for-GitHub was pure redundant surface + a third-party dependency. The Composio/GitHub framing came from the EXPIRED BuilderShip hackathon (sponsor), not a technical need — exactly the stale-premise case the new relevance-check aim (8197b533) targets. The effector-layer CONCEPT (real-world hands) only earns its keep for OAuth-gated SaaS with no local CLI (email/calendar/payments). Human-dependent: Felix decides if/when there's a concrete real-world surface worth it, and prefers native CLIs/targeted MCPs over a broad aggregator. GitHub effecting = use `gh` (already available).
